title: carex limosa var. irrigua
intro: Carex limosa var. irrigua, also known as the swamp sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae) and is native to wetlands in North America. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling, prevent infection, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Carex limosa var. irrigua include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Carex limosa var. irrigua is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in salves and poultices for topical application.
